direction_changes.prior_hypothesis: The three most important architectural decisions were CRI, CRDs, and Server-Side Apply.
direction_changes.consult_output: consult_001 returned contradiction/tension pressure around the baseline ranking; consult_004 returned CSI-specific motif and pressure lineage, including inside_voice_runtime:6618eceaa43863e083b2 and CONFLICTING_PATHWAY_REINFORCEMENT.
direction_changes.post_consult_hypothesis: The final third decision should be CSI/in-tree storage plugin migration, with Server-Side Apply demoted to important-but-not-top-three.
direction_changes.verification_outcome: Verified. Public Kubernetes CSI sources support in-tree plugin pain, FlexVolume limitations, CSI GA, and migration behind stable PV/PVC/StorageClass APIs.

direction_changes.prior_hypothesis: The architecture history could be treated as isolated major features: runtime, API extension, apply semantics.
direction_changes.consult_output: consult_001 and consult_005 emphasized contradiction pressure and task-pathway collision around ranking. The memory_dependency_report records a shift to the motif of moving ecosystem integration points out of core behind stable APIs.
direction_changes.post_consult_hypothesis: The stronger historical explanation is a repeated governance pattern: Kubernetes keeps stable APIs in core while moving runtime/storage/provider implementation outside core.
evidence_discovered: CRI/dockershim evidence reinterpreted as runtime extraction.
CSI migration evidence added as storage extraction.
CRD/API aggregation evidence retained as API extension boundary.
direction_changes.verification_outcome: Verified as a synthetic architectural interpretation grounded in public source lineages; not a standalone historical fact from consultation.

non_direction_changes.reason: Confirmed and bounded an existing baseline conclusion.
Confirmed and bounded an existing baseline conclusion.
Audited claim boundaries after the main direction change had already occurred.
